slides.headline: A composer we spoke to in Month 2 had three confirmed

slides.body:
Music already aired. Already used. Fees already paid. THE MOST COMMON MISTAKE:
Signing a label deal that includes a publishing clause without understanding it.

But he had never registered publishing before the licences were signed. One sync fee pays once.
Published royalties from a single placement can pay for years.
Estimated performance royalties gone: over ₦800K. Uncollectable. Permanently. Don't leave the second half of the deal unregistered.
Here's the publishing rights breakdown nobody teaches in Lagos. ↓

WHAT PUBLISHING RIGHTS ACTUALLY CONTROL:
→ Who collects your performance royalties every time the content airs
→ Who approves sync licence requests — if they can

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
